name: footnote
role: Container pattern. Holds the inline trigger and the anchored content. Four position variants control where the content reveals.

semanticStructure: Content body. May contain phrasing or small block content.

positionContracts.sidenote-margin: Content rendered alongside the prose in the page's outer margin. Uses CSS Anchor Positioning (anchor-name on trigger, position-anchor on content). Falls back to footnote-bottom if anchor positioning is unavailable AND the polyfill is not loaded.
positionContracts.footnote-bottom: Content rendered at the bottom of the prose block (the classic register). Trigger is a superscript link; content is collected at document end. Works without any new platform features.
positionContracts.popup-on-hover: Content rendered as a popover anchored to the trigger; appears on hover (and focus, for keyboard). Uses [popover] attribute + CSS Anchor Positioning. Degrades to footnote-bottom when popover is unavailable.
positionContracts.popup-on-click: Content rendered as a popover; appears on click/Enter/Space and dismisses on outside-click or Esc. Same platform requirements as popup-on-hover.

ariaPatterns.trigger: Anchor with href to the content's id. Uses aria-describedby to associate trigger with content for assistive tech, so screen readers can surface the footnote when the cursor lands on the trigger.
ariaPatterns.content: <aside role='note'> for prose footnotes. Use role='doc-footnote' when DPub-ARIA is in scope (digital publishing applications).
ariaPatterns.popoverRoleNote: For popup-on-hover/popup-on-click, the popover element gets role='note' or role='dialog' depending on whether the operator needs the content to trap focus. Default is 'note' (non-modal); 'dialog' only when the content has its own actions inside.

meta.anchorPositioningSupport: Baseline January 2026 (Chrome 125+, Safari 18.2+, Firefox 128+). For older browsers, load the OddBird polyfill before any sidenote-margin or popup-* variant is rendered. See pack README § Polyfill.
meta.halt: If a consumer needs scroll-tracking semantics (e.g., footnote auto-collapses when out of viewport), that requires a separate primitive (out of scope for v1.0). v1.0 ships static anchor positioning only.

name: footnote-trigger
role: Inline marker (typically a superscript number or symbol) that anchors to the content. Always an interactive anchor element with href.

ariaPatterns.ariaLabel: Provide aria-label='Footnote {n}' so the marker is announced fully (numeric markers alone are noisy for screen readers).
ariaPatterns.ariaDescribedby: Required — links trigger to content so AT users get the footnote body when focusing the trigger.

meta.interaction: Activates the associated content. For popup-on-click variant: invokes showPopover() on the content. For sidenote-margin / footnote-bottom: standard anchor-jump (scroll to content).

name: footnote-content
role: The footnote body. Anchored to the trigger via id reference. Rendered in the position implied by the parent's data-position.

semanticStructure: Footnote body content. Phrasing or short block content.

cssAnchorPositioning.polyfillNote: The OddBird polyfill transparently handles these declarations on older browsers. Detection: @supports (anchor-name: --x) {} — when unsupported, conditionally load the polyfill script.

ariaPatterns.roleNote: role='note' is the right semantic for prose footnotes. role='doc-footnote' is a DPub-ARIA alternative used in formal digital publishing.
ariaPatterns.backReference: Include a back-reference link with aria-label='Back to text' so AT users can return to the trigger after reading the footnote.
